headlines.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after ‘brief and sudden illness’
headlines.description: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who was elected to the Senate in 2003 and was a close political ally of President Donald Trump, has died, his office confirmed early Sunday. He was 71.

headlines.title: Graham Platner officially withdraws from the Maine Senate race
headlines.description: Embattled candidate Graham Platner officially dropped out of Maine’s U.S.

headlines.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 from a 'sudden illness'
headlines.description: Republican South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham has died at 71 from a "brief and sudden illness." Emergency personnel responded to a call for "cardiac arrest" at Graham's Capitol Hill home.

headlines.title: Iran’s supreme leader vows revenge for father’s killing
headlines.description: Iranian state media reports that the Revolutionary Guard says the Strait of Hormuz is closed until further notice. It comes as Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is vowing revenge for his father’s killing, saying it is the “demand” of his country. Khamenei has not been seen since he was severely injured when the war began. His father was killed early in the war by U.S. and Israeli air strikes. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin has the latest.

headlines.title: Rep. Ro Khanna says he was detained by Israeli settlers during West Bank visit
headlines.description: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., said his group’s van was surrounded by Israeli settlers armed with machine guns, while visiting an abandoned Palestinian village in the West Bank. An aide for Khanna said the group was held for over an hour before officers who appeared to be police allowed them to proceed.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Judge tosses remaining Jan. 6 Proud Boys convictions after DOJ request
headlines.description: The convictions against the four Proud Boys are some of the last remaining cases to stand after President Donald Trump’s sweeping pardons from January 2025.

headlines.title: On the same day, a Florida airport and a Tennessee bridge are renamed after Trump
headlines.description: Palm Beach International Airport is now President Donald J. Trump International Airport, and a Jefferson County bridge is the President Donald J. Trump Bridge.

headlines.title: Trump ousts remaining members of the Election Assistance Commission
headlines.description: The dismissals hamstring a key bipartisan agency just months before the midterms.

headlines.title: Ministry founder Victor Marx wins Republican primary for Colorado governor
headlines.description: Marx, who has gained notice due to a claim he killed a man when he was a child, will advance to the general election, NBC News projects.

headlines.title: Democrats in mad dash to replace Graham Platner before deadline
headlines.description: Campaign volunteers have been taping over old Platner signs to quickly repurpose them for new candidates.

headlines.title: Judge orders release of the $5.8 million payment Trump owed E. Jean Carroll
headlines.description: The move came after the president’s attorneys launched a long-shot bid to the Supreme Court to reconsider its decision not to hear his appeal.
